3: require 'active_record'
4: require 'logger'
5: 
6: module Repositories
7:   class BaseRepository
8:     attr_reader :model_class, :logger
9: 
10:     def initialize(model_class)
11:       @model_class = model_class
12:       @logger = Logger.new($stdout)
13:       @cache = {}
14:     end
15: 
16:     def where(filters = {})
17:       scope = model_class.all
18:       filters.each do |key, value|
19:         scope = scope.where(key => value)
20:       end
21:       scope
22:     end
23: 
24:     def find_by_id(id)
25:       cached = @cache[id]
26:       return cached if cached
27: 
28:       record = model_class.find(id)
29:       @cache[id] = record
30:       record
31:     end
32: 
33:     def create(attributes)
34:       record = model_class.create!(attributes)
35:       @cache[record.id] = record
36:       record
37:     end
38: 
39:     def update(id, attributes)
40:       record = find_by_id(id)
41:       record.update!(attributes)
42:       @cache[id] = record
43:       record
44:     end
45: 
46:     def delete(id)
47:       record = model_class.find(id)
48:       record.destroy
49:       @cache.delete(id)
50:       true
51:     rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
52:       false
53:     end
54: 
55:     def count(filters = {})
56:       where(filters).count
57:     end
58: 
59:     def exists?(filters = {})
60:       where(filters).exists?
61:     end
62: 
63:     def find_or_create(attributes)
64:       record = where(attributes).first
65:       return record if record
66: 
67:       create(attributes)
68:     end
69: 
70:     def paginate(filters = {}, page: 1, per_page: 25)
71:       scope = where(filters)
72:       total = scope.count
73:       records = scope.offset((page - 1) * per_page).limit(per_page)
74:       {
75:         records: records.to_a,
76:         total: total,
77:         page: page,
78:         per_page: per_page,
79:         total_pages: (total.to_f / per_page).ceil
80:       }
81:     end
82: 
83:     def batch_find(ids)
84:       ids.map { |id| find_by_id(id) }
85:     end
86: 
87:     def refresh_cache(id)
88:       @cache.delete(id)
89:       find_by_id(id)
90:     end
91: 
92:     private
93: 
94:     def log_query(action, filters)
95:       logger.debug("[#{model_class.name}] #{action}: #{filters.inspect}")
96:     end
97:   end
98: end

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The method find_all was renamed to where on line 16. Consumer app/services/order_service.rb calls @repo.find_all(status: 'pending') on lines 7 and 13. Since find_all no longer exists on BaseRepository, both calls will raise NoMethodError at runtime. OrderService must be updated to call @repo.where() instead.
suggestionsToEvaluate.existingCode:
def where(filters = {})
      scope = model_class.all
      filters.each do |key, value|
        scope = scope.where(key => value)
      end
      scope
    end
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
def where(filters = {})
      scope = model_class.all
      filters.each do |key, value|
        scope = scope.where(key => value)
      end
      scope
    end

    # Preserve backward-compatible alias
    alias_method :find_all, :where
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Renaming find_all to where breaks OrderService which calls find_all on lines 7 and 13
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The find_by_id method on line 24 now uses model_class.find(id) which raises ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound when the record does not exist. Previously it used find_by(id: id) which returns nil. Consumer app/controllers/api_controller.rb checks 'if record.nil?' on line 4 to render a 404 response. With the new implementation, find_by_id will raise RecordNotFound before the nil check is reached, resulting in an unhandled 500 error instead of the intended 404 JSON response.
suggestionsToEvaluate.existingCode:
def find_by_id(id)
      cached = @cache[id]
      return cached if cached

      record = model_class.find(id)
      @cache[id] = record
      record
    end
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
def find_by_id(id)
      cached = @cache[id]
      return cached if cached

      record = model_class.find_by(id: id)
      @cache[id] = record if record
      record
    end
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: find_by_id now raises RecordNotFound instead of returning nil, breaking api_controller.rb nil check on line 4
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The rename of find_all to where on line 16 breaks callers that still reference the old method name. This will cause NoMethodError at runtime in any service that uses find_all to query records. The affected callers should be updated to use where() instead.
suggestionsToEvaluate.existingCode:
def where(filters = {})
      scope = model_class.all
      filters.each do |key, value|
        scope = scope.where(key => value)
      end
      scope
    end
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
def where(filters = {})
      scope = model_class.all
      filters.each do |key, value|
        scope = scope.where(key => value)
      end
      scope
    end

    alias_method :find_all, :where
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Renaming find_all to where will cause NoMethodError in callers still using the old method name
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The batch_find method on line 83 calls find_by_id for each ID sequentially using ids.map { |id| find_by_id(id) }. This produces N+1 individual SELECT queries (one per ID). For a batch of 100 IDs, this means 100 separate database round-trips instead of a single WHERE id IN (...) query. The method should use model_class.where(id: ids) to load all records in a single query, then populate the cache from the result set.
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: batch_find executes N+1 individual queries instead of a single WHERE id IN query
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

expectedActions.reason: Suggestion correctly identifies find_all rename breaking OrderService with specific file path and line numbers, but fails to mention that UserService in app/services/user_service.rb also calls @repo.find_all() on lines 7 and 13 and is equally broken by the same rename.
Correctly identifies find_by_id behavior change breaking api_controller.rb nil check with specific file path and line number.

Suggestion correctly identifies the find_all rename will cause NoMethodError but describes the impact generically ('any service that uses find_all') without naming the two specific affected files visible in cross-file snippets: UserService in app/services/user_service.rb and OrderService in app/services/order_service.rb.
Valid N+1 query performance bug in batch_find with accurate analysis and correct fix using WHERE id IN.

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The Configuration module was refactored from module constants to instance methods on a Singleton class. Consumer app/connectors/database_connector.rb references Configuration::DATABASE_URL on line 3 and Configuration::MAX_CONNECTIONS on line 4. Since these constants no longer exist, both references will raise NameError: uninitialized constant at runtime. DatabaseConnector must use Configuration.instance.database_url and Configuration.instance.max_connections instead.
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
DATABASE_URL = instance.database_url
  MAX_CONNECTIONS = instance.max_connections

  def database_url
    @settings[:database_url] || ENV['DATABASE_URL']
  end
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Removing module constants breaks database_connector.rb which references Configuration::DATABASE_URL and MAX_CONNECTIONS
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The removal of module-level constants like DATABASE_URL, REDIS_URL, SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, and MAX_CONNECTIONS will break any consumer that references them via Configuration::CONSTANT_NAME. These constants were replaced with instance methods on a Singleton class, so all callers will get NameError at runtime. Callers should migrate to using Configuration.instance.method_name instead.
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
# Backward-compatible constant aliases
  DATABASE_URL = instance.database_url
  REDIS_URL = instance.redis_url
  SMTP_HOST = instance.smtp_host
  SMTP_PORT = instance.smtp_port
  MAX_CONNECTIONS = instance.max_connections

  def database_url
    @settings[:database_url] || ENV['DATABASE_URL']
  end
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Removing module constants will raise NameError in callers that reference Configuration::CONSTANT_NAME
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The feature_enabled? method on line 33 now includes the guard 'return false unless flag.is_a?(Symbol)', which silently returns false when a string is passed. Consumer app/services/feature_gate.rb calls config.feature_enabled?(feature_name.to_s) on line 4, explicitly converting the argument to a string. Additionally, gate_request on line 12 passes the literal string "dark_mode". Both calls will silently return false even when the feature is enabled, because the Symbol type check rejects strings. The guard should accept both strings and symbols.
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
def feature_enabled?(flag)
    flag = flag.to_sym if flag.is_a?(String)

    features = @settings.fetch(:features, {})
    features.fetch(flag, false)
  end
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: feature_enabled? rejects string arguments but feature_gate.rb passes strings on lines 4 and 12
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The load_from_file method on line 74 calls ERB.new(File.read(path)).result to process the config YAML file through ERB templating. This allows arbitrary Ruby code execution via ERB tags embedded in the YAML config file. If an attacker can modify app_config.yml, they can execute arbitrary system commands. The ERB evaluation should be restricted or removed.
suggestionsToEvaluate.existingCode:
def load_from_file
    path = File.join(Rails.root, 'config', 'app_config.yml')
    content = ERB.new(File.read(path)).result
    yaml = YAML.safe_load(content, symbolize_names: true)
    @settings.merge!(yaml)
  end
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
def load_from_file
    path = File.join(Rails.root, 'config', 'app_config.yml')
    content = File.read(path)
    yaml = YAML.safe_load(content, symbolize_names: true)
    @settings.merge!(yaml)
  end
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: ERB.new(...).result in load_from_file allows arbitrary code execution via config file injection
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

expectedActions.reason: Suggestion correctly identifies constant removal breaking database_connector.rb with specific file path and NameError, but fails to mention that redis_client.rb (Configuration::REDIS_URL on line 5) and mailer.rb (Configuration::SMTP_HOST on line 5 and Configuration::SMTP_PORT on line 6) are also broken by the same constant removal.

Suggestion correctly identifies the constant removal will break callers with NameError but describes the impact generically ('any consumer that references them') without naming the three specific affected files visible in cross-file snippets: database_connector.rb, redis_client.rb, and mailer.rb.
Correctly identifies Symbol type guard breaking feature_gate.rb which passes string arguments on specific lines.
Valid security finding about ERB template evaluation enabling arbitrary code execution via config file.

10: 
11:    def fields_registry
12:      @fields_registry ||= []
13:    end
14: 
15:    def has_many(association, serializer:)
16:      associations_registry << { name: association, serializer: serializer, type: :has_many }
17:    end
18: 
19:    def belongs_to(association, serializer:)
20:      associations_registry << { name: association, serializer: serializer, type: :belongs_to }
21:    end
22: 
23:    def associations_registry
24:      @associations_registry ||= []
25:    end
26:  end
27: 
28:  def initialize(object, depth: 0)
29:    @object = object
30:    @depth = depth
31:  end
32: 
33:  def serialize(object = @object)
34:    result = {}
35: 
36:    self.class.fields_registry.each do |field_def|
37:      name = field_def[:name]
38:      value = field_def[:block] ? field_def[:block].call(object) : object.send(name)
39:      result[name] = cast_value(value, field_def[:type])
40:    end
41: 
42:    self.class.associations_registry.each do |assoc|
43:      serializer_class = assoc[:serializer]
44:      if assoc[:type] == :has_many
45:        related = object.send(assoc[:name])
46:        result[assoc[:name]] = related.map do |item|
47:          serializer_class.new(item, depth: @depth + 1).serialize
48:        end
49:      else
50:        related = object.send(assoc[:name])
51:        result[assoc[:name]] = serializer_class.new(related, depth: @depth + 1).serialize if related
52:      end
53:    end
54: 
55:    result.to_json
56:  end
57: 
58:  def method_missing(method_name, *args)
59:    if @object.respond_to?(method_name)
60:      @object.send(method_name, *args)
61:    else
62:      super
63:    end
64:  end
65: 
66:  private
67: 
68:  def cast_value(value, type)
69:    case type
70:    when :string then value.to_s
71:    when :integer then value.to_i
72:    when :float then value.to_f
73:    when :boolean then !!value
74:    when :datetime then value&.iso8601
75:    else value
76:    end
77:  end
78: 
79:  def max_depth_reached?
80:    @depth > 3
81:  end
82: 
83:  def serialize_association(object, serializer_class)
84:    return { id: object.id } if max_depth_reached?
85:    serializer_class.new(object, depth: @depth + 1).serialize
86:  end
87: end

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The attributes class method was replaced by the field method on line 7. Consumer app/serializers/user_serializer.rb uses 'attributes :id, :name, :email, :role, :created_at' on line 2. Since the attributes class method no longer exists on SerializerBase, UserSerializer will raise NoMethodError: undefined method 'attributes' for UserSerializer:Class at class load time. UserSerializer must be updated to use the new field DSL.
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Removing attributes macro breaks user_serializer.rb which uses attributes on line 2
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The serialize method on line 33 now returns a JSON string via result.to_json on line 55 instead of a Hash. Consumer app/controllers/api_controller.rb calls 'render json: serializer.serialize(user)' on lines 5 and 10. Rails' render json: internally calls .to_json on the argument. When the argument is already a JSON string, .to_json wraps it in quotes and escapes internal quotes, producing double-encoded output. All API responses will return malformed JSON to clients.
suggestionsToEvaluate.existingCode:
result.to_json
  end
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
result
  end
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: serialize returns JSON string instead of Hash causing double-encoding in api_controller.rb render json: calls
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: UserSerializer defines 'has_many :orders, serializer: OrderSerializer' on line 4, and OrderSerializer defines 'belongs_to :user, serializer: UserSerializer' on line 4. The associations_registry loop on lines 42-53 in serialize() recursively creates new serializer instances without checking depth. The max_depth_reached? method exists on line 79 and the serialize_association helper on line 83 uses it, but the main serialize loop calls serializer_class.new(item).serialize directly instead of using serialize_association. This results in SystemStackError: stack level too deep.
suggestionsToEvaluate.existingCode:
self.class.associations_registry.each do |assoc|
      serializer_class = assoc[:serializer]
      if assoc[:type] == :has_many
        related = object.send(assoc[:name])
        result[assoc[:name]] = related.map do |item|
          serializer_class.new(item, depth: @depth + 1).serialize
        end
      else
        related = object.send(assoc[:name])
        result[assoc[:name]] = serializer_class.new(related, depth: @depth + 1).serialize if related
      end
    end
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
self.class.associations_registry.each do |assoc|
      next if max_depth_reached?

      serializer_class = assoc[:serializer]
      if assoc[:type] == :has_many
        related = object.send(assoc[:name])
        result[assoc[:name]] = related.map do |item|
          serialize_association(item, serializer_class)
        end
      else
        related = object.send(assoc[:name])
        result[assoc[:name]] = serialize_association(related, serializer_class) if related
      end
    end
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Circular has_many/belongs_to between UserSerializer and OrderSerializer causes infinite recursion
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The removal of the attributes class method will break subclasses that still use the old DSL. Since attributes is called at class load time, any serializer subclass that calls 'attributes :id, :name, ...' will crash with NoMethodError before any request is processed. The affected serializers need to migrate to the new field method.
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Removing attributes macro will crash serializer subclasses that use the old DSL at class load time
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

expectedActions.reason: Suggestion correctly identifies attributes macro removal breaking user_serializer.rb with specific file path and NoMethodError, but fails to mention that order_serializer.rb also uses 'attributes :id, :total, :status, :created_at' on line 2 and is equally broken by the same removal.
Correctly identifies serialize returning JSON string causing double-encoding in api_controller.rb render json: on specific lines.
Correctly identifies infinite recursion between UserSerializer and OrderSerializer with max_depth_reached? not being used in the main loop.

Suggestion correctly identifies the attributes macro removal will break subclasses but describes the impact generically ('any serializer subclass') without naming the two specific affected files visible in cross-file snippets: user_serializer.rb and order_serializer.rb which both call attributes on line 2.

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: EventBus no longer includes Singleton (removed from old line 6), so EventBus.instance is no longer available. Consumer app/listeners/order_listener.rb calls EventBus.instance on line 3 to get the bus. This will raise NoMethodError: undefined method 'instance' for EventBus:Class. OrderListener must be updated to use EventBus.current instead.
suggestionsToEvaluate.existingCode:
class << self
    def current
      @current ||= new
    end

    def reset!
      @current = nil
    end
  end
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
class << self
    def current
      @current ||= new
    end

    alias_method :instance, :current

    def reset!
      @current = nil
    end
  end
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Removing Singleton breaks order_listener.rb which calls EventBus.instance on line 3
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The subscribe method on line 48 was changed from subscribe(event_name, &block) to subscribe(event_class, handler_method), now requiring an Event class and a method reference instead of a string name with a block. Consumer app/services/checkout_service.rb calls EventBus.current.publish('order.completed', { ... }) on line 4 and EventBus.current.publish('order.cancelled', { order_id: order.id }) on line 13, passing string event names and hash payloads. The publish method on line 60 validates 'unless event.is_a?(Events::BaseEvent)' and will raise ArgumentError because a String is not an Events::BaseEvent instance. CheckoutService must create proper Event instances to publish.
suggestionsToEvaluate.existingCode:
def publish(event)
    unless event.is_a?(Events::BaseEvent)
      raise ArgumentError, "Expected an Event instance, got #{event.class}"
    end

    logger.info("Publishing #{event.name} at #{event.timestamp}")
    subscribers = @subscribers[event.class]
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: publish now requires Event instances but checkout_service.rb passes string + hash on lines 4 and 13
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The removal of EventBus.instance will break any consumer that uses the old Singleton pattern to access the bus. Since EventBus now uses a class-level .current method instead, callers referencing .instance will get NoMethodError. All listeners and handlers that use EventBus.instance must migrate to EventBus.current.
suggestionsToEvaluate.existingCode:
class << self
    def current
      @current ||= new
    end

    def reset!
      @current = nil
    end
  end
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
class << self
    def current
      @current ||= new
    end

    alias_method :instance, :current

    def reset!
      @current = nil
    end
  end
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Removing Singleton causes NoMethodError for any consumer calling EventBus.instance
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The publish method on lines 68-70 iterates over @subscribers[event.class] and calls handler.call(event) directly without error isolation. If the first subscriber's handler raises an exception, the .each loop aborts and remaining subscribers are never notified. A private safe_call method exists on lines 97-101 that wraps calls in a rescue, but publish uses handler.call(event) directly instead of safe_call(handler, event). One failing handler silently prevents all subsequent handlers from receiving the event.
suggestionsToEvaluate.existingCode:
subscribers.each do |handler|
      handler.call(event)
    end
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
subscribers.each do |handler|
      safe_call(handler, event)
    end
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: No error isolation in publish loop means one failing handler prevents all subsequent handlers from being called
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

expectedActions.reason: Suggestion correctly identifies Singleton removal breaking order_listener.rb with specific file path and NoMethodError, but fails to mention that inventory_listener.rb (EventBus.instance on line 3) and notification_handler.rb (EventBus.instance on lines 3 and 7) are also broken by the same Singleton removal.
Correctly identifies publish type requirement breaking checkout_service.rb with specific file path, both affected lines, and ArgumentError.

Suggestion correctly identifies the Singleton removal will break callers using EventBus.instance but describes the impact generically ('any consumer that uses the old Singleton pattern') without naming the three specific affected files visible in cross-file snippets: order_listener.rb, inventory_listener.rb, and notification_handler.rb.
Valid error isolation bug with safe_call method existing but unused in the publish loop.

<- CUT CONTENT ->
3: require 'faraday'
4: require 'faraday/retry'
5: require 'json'
6: require 'logger'
7: 
8: class HttpClient
9:   attr_reader :base_url, :logger
10: 
11:  def initialize(base_url, headers: {})
12:    @base_url = base_url
13:    @logger = Logger.new($stdout)
14:    @default_headers = {
15:      'Content-Type' => 'application/json',
16:      'Accept' => 'application/json'
17:    }.merge(headers)
18:    @connection = build_connection
19:  end
20: 
21:  def get(url, headers: {})
22:    merged = @default_headers.merge(headers)
23:    logger.debug("GET #{base_url}#{url}")
24:    @connection.get(url) do |req|
25:      req.headers = merged
26:    end
27:  end
28: 
29:  def post(url, body, headers = {})
30:    merged = @default_headers.merge(headers)
31:    logger.debug("POST #{base_url}#{url}")
32:    @connection.post(url) do |req|
33:      req.headers = merged
34:      req.body = body.is_a?(String) ? body : body.to_json
35:    end
36:  end
37: 
38:  def put(url, body, headers = {})
39:    merged = @default_headers.merge(headers)

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The get method on line 21 now returns a Faraday::Response instead of Net::HTTPResponse. Consumer app/gateways/api_gateway.rb calls response.code on lines 8 and 16, comparing against the string '200'. Faraday::Response does not have a .code method -- it uses .status which returns an Integer, not a String. Both response.code calls will raise NoMethodError. Additionally, the Faraday connection configures f.response :json on line 76 which auto-parses the body, so JSON.parse(response.body) on lines 11 and 17 will raise TypeError because the body is already a Hash.
suggestionsToEvaluate.existingCode:
def get(url, headers: {})
    merged = @default_headers.merge(headers)
    logger.debug("GET #{base_url}#{url}")
    @connection.get(url) do |req|
      req.headers = merged
    end
  end
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
def get(url, headers: {})
    merged = @default_headers.merge(headers)
    logger.debug("GET #{base_url}#{url}")
    response = @connection.get(url) do |req|
      req.headers = merged
    end
    adapt_response(response)
  end
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Faraday::Response lacks .code method breaking api_gateway.rb on lines 8 and 16, plus auto-parsed body causes TypeError
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The switch from Net::HTTP to Faraday changes the response object type returned by all HTTP methods. Faraday::Response uses .status (Integer) instead of .code (String) and auto-parses JSON bodies via the :json middleware. Any consumer that checks response.code or manually calls JSON.parse on the body will break. The callers need to be updated to use response.status and access response.body directly as a parsed object.
suggestionsToEvaluate.existingCode:
def get(url, headers: {})
    merged = @default_headers.merge(headers)
    logger.debug("GET #{base_url}#{url}")
    @connection.get(url) do |req|
      req.headers = merged
    end
  end
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
def get(url, headers: {})
    merged = @default_headers.merge(headers)
    logger.debug("GET #{base_url}#{url}")
    response = @connection.get(url) do |req|
      req.headers = merged
    end
    response.define_singleton_method(:code) { status.to_s }
    response
  end
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Faraday::Response has different API than Net::HTTPResponse breaking callers that use .code or JSON.parse on body
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The post method on line 29 changed its signature from post(url, body:, headers: {}) with keyword arguments to post(url, body, headers = {}) with positional arguments. Consumer app/services/webhook_sender.rb calls @client.post('/webhooks', body: payload, headers: auth_headers) on line 13 using the old keyword syntax. With the new positional signature, Ruby interprets {body: payload, headers: auth_headers} as a single Hash positional argument for body, and headers defaults to {}. The actual payload is wrapped in an extra Hash with :body and :headers keys, and the HMAC signature headers are lost. Webhooks will be sent with malformed payloads and no authentication.
suggestionsToEvaluate.existingCode:
def post(url, body, headers = {})
    merged = @default_headers.merge(headers)
    logger.debug("POST #{base_url}#{url}")
    @connection.post(url) do |req|
      req.headers = merged
      req.body = body.is_a?(String) ? body : body.to_json
    end
  end
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
def post(url, body = nil, headers: {})
    merged = @default_headers.merge(headers)
    logger.debug("POST #{base_url}#{url}")
    @connection.post(url) do |req|
      req.headers = merged
      req.body = body.is_a?(String) ? body : body.to_json
    end
  end
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: post changed from keyword body: to positional breaking webhook_sender.rb keyword calls on line 13
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The Faraday::Response .code incompatibility affects health checking functionality. Consumer lib/health_checker.rb calls response.code on line 11 comparing against '200', and calls response.code.to_i on line 18 checking if status is less than 400. Since Faraday::Response uses .status (Integer) instead of .code (String), both health check methods will crash with NoMethodError. The health monitoring system will report false failures for all services.
suggestionsToEvaluate.existingCode:
def get(url, headers: {})
    merged = @default_headers.merge(headers)
    logger.debug("GET #{base_url}#{url}")
    @connection.get(url) do |req|
      req.headers = merged
    end
  end
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
def get(url, headers: {})
    merged = @default_headers.merge(headers)
    logger.debug("GET #{base_url}#{url}")
    response = @connection.get(url) do |req|
      req.headers = merged
    end
    response.define_singleton_method(:code) { status.to_s }
    response
  end
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Faraday::Response lacks .code method breaking health_checker.rb on lines 11 and 18
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

expectedActions.reason: Suggestion correctly identifies Faraday::Response .code incompatibility and auto-parsed body breaking api_gateway.rb with specific lines and both failure modes, but fails to mention that health_checker.rb also calls response.code on lines 11 and 18 and is equally broken by the same response type change.

Suggestion correctly identifies the Faraday response API change will break callers but describes the impact generically ('any consumer that checks response.code or manually calls JSON.parse') without naming the two specific affected files visible in cross-file snippets: api_gateway.rb (response.code on lines 8/16, JSON.parse on lines 11/17) and health_checker.rb (response.code on lines 11/18).
Correctly identifies post keyword-to-positional change breaking webhook_sender.rb with specific file path, line number, and payload corruption.
Correctly identifies Faraday::Response .code incompatibility breaking health_checker.rb with specific file path and both affected lines.
